name: ytdlp-wrapper
description: Server-side wrapper around yt-dlp for Node.js apps. Fetch metadata, search YouTube, resolve playable URLs from any yt-dlp-supported site, dump flat playlists. Cookie-aware with structured error handling.

envVars.name: YTDLP_PATH
envVars.description: Absolute path to the yt-dlp binary. Default: /usr/bin/yt-dlp

envVars.name: YTDLP_COOKIES_PATH
envVars.description: Absolute path to a Netscape-format cookies.txt. Used for sites that require auth (YouTube age-gated, members-only, etc.). Optional.

systemDependencies.name: yt-dlp
systemDependencies.install: pip install yt-dlp  (or apt-get install yt-dlp on Debian/Ubuntu)

systemDependencies.name: node
systemDependencies.note: yt-dlp is invoked with --js-runtimes node so a node binary must be on PATH inside the container/host

endpoints: POST /api/ytdlp/info — fetch metadata for one URL (admin-only, optional)
GET /api/youtube/search?q=... — search YouTube via yt-dlp (signed-in, optional)
GET /api/resolve-url?url=... — resolve to a direct stream URL (signed-in, optional)

postInstall: 1. Install yt-dlp on the host/container (must be on PATH or set YTDLP_PATH)
2. Optionally drop a Netscape cookies.txt at YTDLP_COOKIES_PATH for age-gated/private content
3. The 3 API routes are convenience wrappers — copy only what you need; lib/ytdlp.ts is the core
4. To download (not just resolve), use ytdlpSpawnArgs() + ytdlpBinaryPath() with child_process.spawn — see playlist-import-job and background-job-template for patterns
